findings: The unnumbered ruler at the right has no units or defined scale, so it cannot substantiate the printed measurements.

The caption “Wing-pattern (diagnostic)” is weakly supported and potentially misleading: the illustrated wing lacks an annotated species-specific character, while current identification guidance emphasizes the darker face, broad white moustache, whiter belly, and vocalizations.

All other visible claims are consistent with the checked sources. The adult-male plumage, proportions, bill, eye-ring, tail, legs, feet, taxonomy, measurements, habitat, and diet are credible. The main bird has one head, one beak, two anatomically coherent wings with the far wing naturally obscured, two legs, and one tail.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

verification_sources.title: Eastern Subalpine Warbler — Birds of the World
